injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Targeting Champions League final on 2026-05-30; likely to miss Ecuador's 2026-05-30 friendly vs Saudi Arabia

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Last appearance before PSG ruled him out of matches vs Brest (May 10) and Lens (May 13) as a precaution

injuryUpdates.source: Washington Post / AP - May 9 2026; RotoWire - May 12 2026; Primicias.ec - May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Ruled out of Ligue 1 fixtures vs Brest and Lens with right thigh discomfort; PSG describe it as minor and precautionary ahead of the Champions League final vs Arsenal on 2026-05-30. Pacho is half of Ecuador's first-choice CB pair and will be unavailable for the Saudi Arabia warm-up due to UCL final clash. Fitness for 2026 tournament opener still expected.

injuryUpdates.club: Arsenal (on loan from Bayer Leverkusen; permanent move agreed for summer 2026)
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: N/A - currently fit; will miss Ecuador's 2026-05-30 friendly vs Saudi Arabia due to Champions League final commitment with Arsenal

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Continues to feature for Arsenal in run-in; UCL final on 2026-05-30

injuryUpdates.source: Arseblog, FootballTransfers, Daily Cannon - 2025 to May 2026; Primicias.ec - May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Club update: now at Arsenal (loan from Leverkusen since summer 2025; permanent transfer agreed for end of season for c. EUR 52m). Fit and a regular Arsenal starter. Will miss Ecuador's Saudi Arabia warm-up because of the Champions League final on the same day; expected available for tournament opener.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Recurring muscle issues; rotation player at Milan

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Played most recent international; managed minutes at club

injuryUpdates.source: AC Milan official, El Universo, Bolavip Ecuador - 2025-2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Club update: moved from Brighton to AC Milan on a five-year deal in July 2025 (was previously tracked at Brighton). Has started only ~9 of 19 Serie A games and missed several with muscle/suspension issues; Ecuadorian press flagging lack of rhythm as a concern entering the tournament. No new acute injury since 2026-04-16, status unchanged.

squadChanges.details: Center-back Porozo training at the federation HQ and named in Ecuador's expanded ~36-player list for the pre-tournament friendlies vs Saudi Arabia (2026-05-30) and Guatemala (2026-06-07). Adds CB depth with Pacho/Hincapie tied up by the UCL final until May 30.

bettingOddsUpdate.toQualifyFromGroup: approx -270 implied (model probability ~73% to advance from Group E)
bettingOddsUpdate.source: FanDuel / market aggregators via Squawka, RotoWire, Fox Sports - May 2026
bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Ecuador priced around +9000 (90/1) to win the tournament outright. Group E winner odds c. +310 to +400 with Germany the heavy favourite. Projection models give Ecuador roughly a 73% chance to qualify from the group; specific bookmaker price for group-stage exit not surfaced.

summary: Material change since 2026-04-16 is a fresh right-thigh injury to first-choice center-back Willian Pacho at PSG (out from May 10), with both he and Hincapie set to miss Ecuador's only pre-tournament friendly (Saudi Arabia, May 30) because of the Champions League final on the same date. No matches have been played since the bundled data cut-off, no coach change, and the squad is still being finalised. Worth folding in: club updates for Hincapie (Arsenal, permanent summer move agreed) and Estupinan (AC Milan since July 2025, low minutes flagged in Ecuadorian press).
